theme_notes: Taglines riff on: the Ticketmaster/Live Nation illegal-monopoly jury verdict, Meta raising Quest VR prices (AI chip costs), Lana Del Rey marrying an alligator tour guide, the 400,000-KitKat European heist, NASA Artemis II's broken space toilet, the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire and Strait of Hormuz reopening (oil -10%), UBC engineers dangling a VW Beetle on a cliff face, World Cup 2026 tailgating ban at US venues, a retired English teacher selling his second home to follow soccer, graphene's frictionless electron flow, and the James Webb 'forbidden' planet.
settled: A jury found Ticketmaster guilty of monopoly. Your balance found temporary amnesty.
Balanced — like NASA fixed a broken space toilet: barely, over the phone, in orbit.
400,000 stolen KitKats are unaccounted for in Europe. At least your debt has been located.
youOwe: The World Cup banned tailgating at US venues. This debt has also banned fun.
Electrons in graphene flow without friction. Your repayments do not.
Helicopter-dropped marshmallows in Detroit were more aggressively pursued than this.
Meta raised Quest prices citing AI chip costs. You're not worth a firmware patch.
Lana Del Rey married an alligator tour guide. He at least shows up to things.
This is a holy f—ing airball at fiscal responsibility.
Florida men drove a dead gator on their car roof for taxidermy. That's more initiative.
The Strait of Hormuz is open for tankers. Your wallet remains closed for inspection.
Enough to buy a VW shell and perch it on a cliff for laughs. You chose this instead.
Trump says the Iran deal is 'very close.' Your partner isn't saying that about this.
Artemis II went 252,757 miles and still radioed home. You can't check your balance.
James Webb found a 'forbidden' planet that defies all formation models. So does this debt.
The jury convicted Ticketmaster for running an illegal monopoly. You're the service fee.
Oil fell 10% on ceasefire news. Your credibility fell without any announcement.
Scientists found a superconductor that only works under extreme pressure. Prescient.
A retired English teacher sold his second home to follow the World Cup. You've lost more here.
This debt has outlasted ceasefires, negotiations, and the goodwill of nations.
The KitKat thieves took 400,000 bars and have a better recovery trajectory than you.
theyOwe: Less than what NASA spent fixing a space toilet that still almost broke.
Graphene electrons move without resistance. Their debt does not.
Smaller than a World Cup tailgate fund — and the World Cup already banned those.
Almost what Meta's charging extra for a Quest headset. Overpriced for the content.
Swifties are picking World Cup teams by jersey vibes. They chose a pay date the same way.
Florida men drove a dead alligator across state lines for taxidermy. Their commitment: noted.
Enough to dangle a Volkswagen on a cliff face, like UBC did. Less impressive.
Lebanon got a 10-day ceasefire. Their 'I'll pay you back' has been on day one for months.
Artemis II broke the human distance record from Earth. You two are closing in on that gap.
The Iran negotiations need a broker and a miracle. So does this.
The Strait of Hormuz is now open for all ships. Your bank account is not.
Oil dropped 10% on ceasefire news. Their urgency hasn't found a similar catalyst.
The jury found Live Nation guilty of running an illegal monopoly. Your partner runs a tab.
A retired teacher sold his second home for soccer. Your partner can't Venmo four digits.
James Webb found a planet that breaks all planetary formation rules. So does this balance.
An American ceasefire got oil prices down 10% in one day. No one's brokering this.
UTe2 only superconducts under extreme magnetic fields. Same conditions required for repayment.
400,000 KitKats were stolen and Europe has a task force. You have a partner with read receipts.
